Maria A. Delgado.Diagnosis Codes: M17.11 — Primary osteoarthritis, right knee; M17.12 — Primary osteoarthritis, left knee.
Maria A. Delgado.Conservative Treatments Named: Physical therapy (4 weeks), NSAIDs (naproxen 500mg, 3 months), corticosteroid injection left knee, corticosteroid injection right knee, weight loss counseling.
Maria A. Delgado.Conservative Treatment Duration: 6 weeks of structured outpatient physical therapy completed prior to surgical evaluation, with documented functional assessment at completion.
Maria A. Delgado.Imaging Findings: Weight-bearing AP and lateral X-rays of bilateral knees: Kellgren-Lawrence Grade 3 bilaterally with moderate-to-severe joint space narrowing and marginal osteophyte formation.
Maria A. Delgado.Functional Limitations: Cannot walk more than half a block without stopping; unable to climb stairs without bilateral rail support; significant difficulty with prolonged standing exceeding 10 minutes; unable to perform grocery shopping independently.
Maria A. Delgado.Surgical Approach: Cemented implant, bilateral simultaneous total knee arthroplasty.
Maria A. Delgado.Symptom Duration: 24 months of progressive bilateral knee pain, left worse than right.

Robert Chen.Diagnosis Codes: M75.121 — Complete rotator cuff tear, right shoulder, not specified as traumatic.
Robert Chen.Conservative Treatments Named: Physical therapy (6 weeks rotator cuff protocol), NSAIDs — meloxicam 15mg (6 weeks), subacromial corticosteroid injection under ultrasound guidance (x1).
Robert Chen.Conservative Treatment Duration: 6 weeks of structured rotator cuff strengthening and periscapular stabilization physical therapy completed prior to surgical consultation.
Robert Chen.Imaging Findings: MRI right shoulder (March 2026): full-thickness supraspinatus tear, 2.1 cm AP dimension with mild retraction; mild AC joint degenerative change; no biceps tendon pathology.
Robert Chen.Functional Limitations: Unable to perform overhead lifting beyond shoulder height; significant difficulty with grooming and dressing; sleep disruption due to positional shoulder pain; reduced grip strength limiting daily activities.
Robert Chen.Surgical Approach: Arthroscopic rotator cuff repair using a double-row suture anchor construct via standard posterior and anterolateral portals, with acromioplasty as indicated.
Robert Chen.Symptom Duration: Approximately 4 months of progressive right shoulder pain and weakness following a lifting injury at work, with documented functional decline over the past 6 weeks.

Eleanor Vance.Diagnosis Codes: M51.26 — Intervertebral disc displacement, lumbar region.
Eleanor Vance.Conservative Treatments Named: Physical therapy (6 weeks lumbar stabilization program), chiropractic manipulation (8 visits), epidural steroid injection at L4-L5 (x1), NSAIDs — meloxicam 15mg (6 weeks).
Eleanor Vance.Conservative Treatment Duration: 6 weeks of structured lumbar stabilization physical therapy completed, with documented functional outcome assessment at discharge.
Eleanor Vance.Imaging Findings: MRI lumbar spine: L4-L5 disc herniation with moderate-to-severe foraminal stenosis and evidence of left-sided nerve root compression consistent with neurogenic claudication.
Eleanor Vance.Functional Limitations: Unable to walk more than one city block without severe bilateral leg pain; cannot sit for more than 20 minutes without increasing pain; unable to perform bending, lifting, or prolonged standing.
Eleanor Vance.Surgical Approach: Posterior lumbar interbody fusion (PLIF) at L4-L5 with pedicle screw fixation and interbody cage placement.
Eleanor Vance.Symptom Duration: 6 months of progressively worsening low back pain with bilateral lower extremity radiation, with significant symptom escalation over the past 6 weeks despite conservative management.
